name: Staircase
description: Single-flight staircase that climbs from the floor of an AABB to the next level. Steps rise one block per Y along a chosen horizontal direction (default east), with a fence banister on the open side and a 2x2 landing at the top. Used to connect floors, mezzanines, towers, or rooftops.

required_furniture.rule: One @stairs block per rise step; H total, where H = AABB height - 1.
Banister column of @fence next to each step on the open side.
2x2 patch of @primary at the top of the flight as a safe step-off.

placement_rules: Steps rise along one axis (the `direction` kwarg: east/west/north/south).
Banister on the open side, perpendicular to the direction of travel.
Landing at the top — a 2x2 of @primary at the walking surface above the final stair.
A @primary support pillar fills the gap below each step so the flight stays grounded when dropped into empty space.
When the AABB run length is shorter than the climb, the rise is capped to fit (defensive on narrow 4x4x3 boxes).
lighting_ventilation: Place a @light block on the landing or every 4 steps on the banister side to satisfy mob-spawn rules on Java 1.16.5.

medieval.notes: Wooden flight with oak fence banister — fits cottages and inns.

modern.notes: Smooth stone treads with iron-bar handrail for a clean industrial line.

fantasy.notes: Dark-oak treads with a matching fence railing — suits towers and dungeons.
